title: Pursuit and Stop Stick Deployment Protocols

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1.  Any current written policies, procedures, guidelines, manuals, directives, or training materials related to high-speed vehicle pursuits, including criteria for initiating, continuing, or terminating pursuits.

2.  Any specific policies, procedures, or guidelines regarding the deployment of spike strips, stop sticks, or similar tire-deflation devices during vehicle pursuits, including any restrictions on use (e.g., on interstates, busy highways, or near populated areas), approval requirements, risk assessments, or alternatives considered.

3. Any updates, revisions, or amendments to the above policies from January 1, 2015, to the present.

If any portion of these records is exempt from disclosure, please provide the non-exempt portions with redactions clearly marked, along with the specific statutory exemption cited for each redaction.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Carla McGehee

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Elon Musk Security Clearance Review
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

The final report of the Under Secretary of Defense For Intelligence and Security following its review into Elon Musk's security clearance and his compliance with federal reporting protocols, as recently discussed in the New York Times:  https://www.nytimes.com/2024/12/17/technology/elon-musk-spacex-national-security-reporting.html

Please include any other reports investigating Elon Musk's security clearance.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jacob Silverman
